sections.heading: What is Android Studio?
sections.content: Android Studio is the official IDE for Android development created by Google and based on IntelliJ IDEA. It provides tools for coding, designing layouts, debugging, testing, and building Android apps in one place.

sections.heading: Why Use Android Studio?
items: Smart code editor with Kotlin and Java support
Visual Layout Editor for UI design
Logcat and profiling tools for debugging

sections.heading: Important Android Studio Panels
items: Project Panel – View project files and folders
Editor – Write and edit Kotlin/Java/XML code
Logcat – View runtime logs and errors
Device Manager – Create and manage emulators

rows.purpose: Main module containing app code and resources
Source code for Activities, ViewModels, etc.

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content: Android Studio is the primary tool used to build Android apps. Understanding its interface, project structure, and resource system helps developers efficiently create, test, and manage Android applications.
